name: knowledge
context: Knowledge layer - long-form body, sections, aliases, links, related nodes, backlinks, section-target links, and reading paths
info: Explains how Dojo turns manifests into a navigable knowledge graph for humans and agents. This is the right node to read when you want better search quality, better `/learn` responses, or a more connected live node tree.
triggers: how do backlinks work in dojo
body:
# Knowledge Layer

The knowledge layer is what makes Dojo more than a registry. Nodes can teach through `body`, `sections`, `aliases`, `links`, `related`, and directed wiki-links like [[dojo/spec#knowledge-fields]] or [[dojo/validate/knowledge#actionability]].

In practice, that means an agent can search by natural phrasing, load a focused section, walk backlinks, inspect a local graph, and then pivot into an executable skill like [[dojo/api/query]] or [[dojo/validate]].

sections.title: Body and sections
sections.body: Use `body` for the long explanation and `sections` for addressable chunks. Section order matters because the learn endpoint uses it to build a default reading path.

sections.title: Aliases and search
sections.body: Aliases let search match the words people actually use. A node with good aliases, grounded tags, and section text will surface better in both `/v1/search` and `/v1/agent/learn`.

sections.title: Section-target links
sections.body: Links do not have to stop at the node level. A link like `dojo/spec#manifest-core` or `dojo/api#knowledge-routes` gives the graph a more precise waypoint and makes section-level reading paths much more useful.

sections.title: Links, backlinks, and related edges
sections.body: Explicit `links` tell the graph where to go next, `related` expresses softer relationships, and backlinks are derived automatically from outgoing graph references and wiki-links. This is why context nodes should link to the skills a reader is likely to execute next.

sections.title: Learn and graph endpoints
sections.body: Use `/v1/learn/*` for the full knowledge payload, `/v1/backlinks/*` for inbound references, `/v1/graph/*` for neighborhood inspection, and `/v1/alias/:alias` when you need to resolve human phrasing. See [[dojo/api#knowledge-routes]] and then execute [[dojo/api/query]] if you need the read-side client behavior.

sections.title: Authoring patterns
sections.body: A strong knowledge node starts with a short `context`, expands in `info`, teaches in `body`, slices into `sections`, points forward with `links`, and gives a reader at least one executable next step. Run [[dojo/validate/knowledge]] before release to catch thin authoring.

links.context: Formal definition of the knowledge fields
HTTP endpoints that expose the knowledge graph
Executable client for search and learn flows
Checks that a manifest is strong enough to teach and act on

related.note: The spec defines the fields the knowledge layer uses
Architecture explains how knowledge is indexed and assembled

author: dojo-community
